description_long: This is a rectangular, industrial street cabinet made of weathered metal, likely galvanized steel, with a utilitarian design. The front features two vertically-grooved doors, one of which has a visible handle and a cylinder lock, and a small sticker or label in the upper right corner. The cabinet sits on a slightly raised base, and its surface shows signs of exposure to the elements, with patches of discoloration and some moss or grime near the bottom. The sides are plain, with subtle panel lines and no visible openings or features. The top is flat and solid, suitable for supporting small objects. This type of cabinet is commonly used to house electrical or telecommunications equipment in urban environments.
description: A weathered, metal street cabinet with two grooved front doors, a lock, and a flat top, typically used for housing utilities in urban areas.
description_view_0: Front view showing two vertically-grooved doors, a handle with a lock, and a small label in the upper right corner.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain, flat metal panel with subtle discoloration and no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view with two plain, grooved metal panels and some moss or grime near the base, lacking handles or labels.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a plain, flat metal panel similar to the left side, with no distinguishing features.
